info.title: Last.app API
info.description:
# Introduction Your integration is going to be linked with a Location, which belongs to an Organization. Each location can have more than one brand (Sushi, Poke, Burgers, ...). And each brand can have more than one catalog, with a different set of products. When you create a new Tab, it has to be linked to a specific brand (brand ids are unique, the same brand in different locations has a different id). Then we will route the tab to the right location under the right brand name. Please go to our developer portal to add your integration. # Authentication For both the Rest API and the webhooks we use the same authentication method. We will share with you an unique and secret token as a integrator, and in all the requests it has to be included as a header. `Authorization: Bearer \${token}` [How to get it?](#tag/FAQ1) You should also include in every endpoint (except this [one](#tag/API-Organizations/operation/OrganizationList), an extra header LocationID or OrganizationID. Look in every endpoint in AUTHORIZATIONS Field to know which one should be added. # Rate limits There are 2 rate limits applying to all endpoints: - Rate limit of 1500 reqs every 10 min indexed by token / entity.
- Rate limit of 15 reqs every second indexed by token / entity.
And there is an specific rate limit only applying to [/organizations](#tag/API-Organizations/operation/OrganizationList) endpoint: - Rate limit of 1 reqs every second indexed by token.
Being an entity a `LocationId` or `OrganizationId` which should be added as a header. Look in every endpoint in AUTHORIZATIONS Field to know which one should be added. # Migration Guide Import changes if you come from V1: - We have defined for most entities 2 schemes to be shared with you: *Simple scheme* and *Complete scheme*.
Note that **GET routes which returns a LIST** are returning entities with **simple scheme** format.
On the other hand, **GET routes which returns a SINGLE ELEMENT** are returning entities with **complete scheme** format.
The first example of this new behaviour is found on [Locations](#tag/API-Locations).
Look response samples of Location List and Location by ID - [Tokens](#tag/FAQ8) have completely changed:
**On V1**, a token allows an integrator to operate over **an organization.**
So every integrator had a token for every organization.
**On V2**, a token allows an integrator to operate over **ALL his organizations.** # Development tools - Please once you have created your integrator on Developer Portal, go to Store section. There you will be able to create a dummy organization with 1 location to test your integration. This location will be created with a dummy floorplan, catalog, schedules, etc. - Note that once you have created this organization, Store Section will change and you will have 2 direct access to the POS and Admin. - To start testing your integration, remember to go to the admin, to the integrations sections and add your integration. # Integrators - Your integrator should be registered on our Developer Portal. It will have the following statuses: [Test](#tag/FAQ2), Pending and Production. - When your integrator is on **Test / Pending** statuses, only dummy location which you create on Developer Portal or Prod locations where you can login with your developer user, will be able to use it. - When your integrator is on **Prod**, it's gonna be visible by **all our locations on their Admin Marketplace**. So please, apart from selecting your capabilities and webhooks, have a time to edit your integrator on Developer Portal (logo, name, description, ...) Let restaurants know how you can help them in their daily lives! - When a restaurant wants to integrate with you, we ask them: 1) A the name for the integration 2) An externalId (this is their unique identifier on your system, so if needed help restaurants to get it) [Do you have a question?](#tag/FAQ12) # Real Time Loyalty LastApp API supports EXTERNAL customers and promotions. To use this loyalty feature, you must implement the following 2 GET endpoints: [GET Customer by phone number](#operation/getCustomerByPhoneNumber) [GET Customer Promotions by phone number](#operation/getCustomerPromotionsByPhoneNumber) You can specify these endpoints URLs on your integrator configuration on Developer Portal. When the POS tries to link a person to a tab with their phone number: 1) If the customer doesn't exist yet on LastApp, we will call your 1st endpoint to get your customer data. 2) We will call the 2nd endpoint to get the available promotions for this customer.

tags.name: FAQ1
tags.x-displayName: How can I get a testing account and a testing auth token?
tags.description: You need to: 1) Create an account on Last Developers Portal 2) Configurate your integrations and for every one you will get a Token. 3) Go to Store to generate a dummy organization to start testing your integration. If you have any doubt, please contact our support team at hola@last.app.

tags.name: FAQ2
tags.x-displayName: Once I have finished the integration with a testing account, how can I pass to Production?
tags.description:
You should ask for this to integrations@last.app sending us your integrator ID(s), we will check and answer you.
After this is done, you will appear on the Admin page of every LastApp location to be discovered!

tags.name: FAQ3
tags.x-displayName: How can I get the ID of each location?
tags.description:
You should enable location:integrated location:desintegrated, to receive in real time when a location decides to add / delete the integration
with your product. We will send you their locationId and locationName.

tags.name: FAQ4
tags.x-displayName: Which is the difference between Location and brand?
tags.description: In our system, a Location is a Restaurant, in simple words. Each location may want to appear with different brands in their online sales channels. For example, a location named Rick Restaurant can have multiple brands appearing at JustEat and UberEats, for example Rick Chickens, Rick Pizza, and Rick Sushi.

tags.name: FAQ5
tags.x-displayName: Which catalogs is my location using currently?
tags.description: In the response from [GET Location By Id](#tag/API-Locations/operation/GetLocation) you will find a list of brands for each location, and each of this brands contains at the same time a list of catalogs.

tags.name: FAQ6
tags.x-displayName: How can I add a webhook URL or suscribe to a webhook event?
tags.description: This should be done by yourself on the Developer Portal. If you have any doubt, please contact our support team at hola@last.app.

tags.name: FAQ7
tags.x-displayName: It is posible to update products pricing or catalogs through API?
tags.description: This is not possible at the moment, the only thing you can update right now is the [status](#tag/API-Catalogs/operation/EnableCatalogProduct) of a catalog product.

tags.name: FAQ8
tags.description:
You'll only need ONE token for every product you have added on the Developer Portal.
For example, let's imagine you have added a Reservation product and Reporting product.
You will need a token for Reservation, and a token for Reporting, this token will identify your product in our system.

tags.name: FAQ9
tags.x-displayName: Which is the difference between tab, order, and bill?
tags.description:
A tab is a restaurant customer request.
An order is a tab which will not be eaten on the Restaurant, which means is a tab with pickupType = ['delivery', 'ownDelivery', 'takeAway'].
A bill is an invoice, and it belongs to a tab. It contains payments and invoice details.

tags.name: FAQ10
tags.x-displayName: Which is the difference between source and pickupType?
tags.description:
The source is who or where the tab has been created, for example can be Restaurant, Shop, Glovo.
The pickupType specifies for the tabs which are orders, who should come to pick it up.

tags.name: FAQ11
tags.description:
LastApp supports payment integrations, if you have terminals like dataphones or devices with tapToPay, you can add them to the specific location and will appear with the rest of payment methods on the POS.
A part from this terminals, you should listen the webhooks related with payment-requests and use the endpoint to confirm or reject the payment request.

tags.name: FAQ12
tags.x-displayName: I have an API question, who can I ask about it?
tags.description: You can send an email to integrations@last.app, where we will be pleased to help you as mush as we can.

get.description:
Returns a list of tabs.
`NOTE` Maximum interval date: 365 days

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Retrieve tabs with activation time after startDate (in ISO 8601)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Retrieve tabs with activation time after endDate (in ISO 8601)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of tabs to skip.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of tabs to be returned.

parameters.name: tabName
parameters.description: Only retrieve tabs with a specific name

parameters.name: tableId
parameters.description: Only retrieve tabs from tables with this id

parameters.description: Only retrieve tabs from tables with a specific name

parameters.name: open
parameters.description: Only retrieve tabs which are open/closed

parameters.name: customerId

get.description:
Allows to retrieve tab products which are not billed yet.
This endpoint can be helpful if you want flexibility when creating bills.
Check `pendingProducts` on POST Create Bill endpoint.

post.description:
Allows to create a bill for a specific tab.
Default: If `pendingProducts` not specified, bill will be created for all pending products & fees pending to be paid.
In case you want to generate a bill only for specific pending products, use `pendingProducts` array specifying the tabProductIds.
Check Tab pending products list GET endpoint.

get.description:
Returns a list of bills.
Paginated endpoint.
`NOTE` Maximum interval date: 365 days

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: Retrieve bills related to this location

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Retrieve bills with activation time after startDate (in ISO 8601)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Retrieve bills with activation time after endDate (in ISO 8601)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of bills to skip

get.description: Returns a bill by his identifier.

post.description: Adds a payment to an existing bill from an open tab. It is possible for a bill to have multiple partial payments. Once all the tab bills are paid fully, the tab will be closed automatically. If the sum of all the payments is greater than the bill total, the difference will be considered as tip for that bill.

post.description:
Allows to create multiple customers at once.
NOTE that the response of this endpoint it's ONLY about the validity of the data.
If you want to get the created customers,
you should listen customer:created / customer:updated webhooks to map with our IDs.

get.description:
Whenever the POS tries to link a person to a tab with their phone number, if the customer doesn't exist yet on LastApp, we will call this endpoint to get the customer data from your system.
The request will be made via `GET` to your chosen endpoint, which on success should return an object with the following schema.
NOTE: Your system has 5 seconds to answer.

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: Phone number of the customer requested

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: Location where the request is being made

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Organization where the request is being made

get.summary: Get customer promotions by phone number
get.description:
After retrieving the customer, we will call this endpoint to get the available promotions for this customer.
The request will be made via `GET` to your chosen endpoint, which on success should return an object with the following schema.
Only promotions applicable to the given location/organization should be returned.
NOTE: Your system has 5 seconds to answer.

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: Phone number of the customer requested

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: Location where the request is being made. Only promotions related to this location should be returned.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Organization where the request is being made. Only promotions related to this organization should be returned.

promotions.name: Welcome Discount
promotions.description: Get $10 off on your first purchase

post.description:
Whenever there is a content change in our side that may be relevant for an integration we trigger webhook updates.
The request will be made via `POST` to your chosen webhook endpoint, which on success should return an empty response with status `200`.
The update will come in `json` format in the body of the request, wrapped in an `event` object, with a corresponding payload (`data`) with the relevant resource or information.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Specify that the content will be sent as JSON

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: LocationId related (only present if event is related to a location)

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: OrganizationId related (only present if event is related to an organization)

NotFound.description: The specified resource was not found

error.example: Access token is missing or invalid
You have no access to this resource
The specified resource was not found
The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due entity expired

pointsExpense.description: Amount of points that are needed and will be consumed when the promotion is used

discountType.description: Type of the promotion. One of products, 2x1, percentage, currency

discountAmount.description: Amount to be discounted by the promotion, can be a percentage or currency amount depending on the type

allowRepeat.description: If enabled this promotion can be applied multiple times in the same order

accumulated.description: If enabled this promotion can be applied alongside other promotions

code.description: Code that can be used by the customer in the shop to apply the promotion

availableInShop.description: If enabled this promotion can be applied in LastShop

availableInPos.description: If enabled this promotion can be applied in LastPOS

availableInAdmin.description: If enabled this promotion will be shown in LastAdmin

startTime.description: Start time of the promotion in ISO 8601 format

endTime.description: End time of the promotion in ISO 8601 format

discountAmount.description: Amount to be discounted by the promotion, can be a percentage or currency amount in cents depending on the type

organizationId.description: Location IDs where the promotion will take effect

maxRedemptions.description: The amount after which the promotion will expire

minExpense.description: Minimum expense in cents for which the promotion will take effect
